title: Fit Analysis: When to Use AI SDR vs DIY Stack

questions.question: For a solo founder sending 50-150 contacts/day with under $200/month budget, which approach does the lesson recommend?

options.text: DIY stack (Instantly + Apollo + ChatGPT) — the economics don't justify an AI SDR platform
No outreach at all until budget increases

questions.explanation: The decision framework routes founders with 50-150 contacts/day and under $200/month budget to the DIY stack. Most solo founders are better served by a $100-200/month DIY stack because the economics of AI SDR platforms don't work at solo founder scale.

questions.question: What is the real question the lesson says 'everyone gets wrong' about AI SDRs?

options.text: Which AI SDR platform is best?
Should I pay $750-5,000/month for a platform to run AI outreach, or build my own system for $100-200/month?
Is AI better than humans at sales?
When should I hire a human SDR?

questions.explanation: Most founders frame the question as 'AI vs manual' which is a false choice — they're already using AI (ChatGPT counts). The real question is whether the platform premium ($750-5,000/month) justifies itself over a well-configured DIY stack ($100-200/month) that achieves comparable results.

questions.question: At what contact volume does an AI SDR platform start making sense?
questions.explanation: The decision tree shows that AI SDR platforms start making economic sense at 200+ contacts/day with a budget of $750+/month. Below that volume, the manual oversight required negates most of the automation benefit, and the cost premium isn't justified by incremental results.

questions.question: Most solo founders reading the course will be better served by a DIY stack than an AI SDR platform.
questions.explanation: True. The lesson states this directly: 'Most solo founders reading this course will be better served by a $100-200/month DIY stack than a $750-5,000/month AI SDR platform. Not because AI SDRs are bad — they're impressive technology — but because the economics don't work at solo founder scale.'

questions.question: Run through the lesson's decision framework with your actual numbers: daily contact volume, monthly budget, current results, and deal size. Where does the framework place you — DIY, Salesforge-tier, or full AI SDR? Do you agree with its recommendation?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response provides specific numbers for each decision criterion (contact volume, budget, current performance), follows the decision tree logic accurately, and reaches a justified conclusion. Look for honest assessment rather than aspirational numbers.
